cases.title: Gentry et al. v. Presbytery of Calvary
cases.synopsis: The Committee on Judicial Business found Case 1, the complaint of TE Gentry et al. against the Presbytery of Calvary, to be in order and recommended that a commission be appointed to adjudicate the matter.

cases.title: Hathaway et al. v. Presbytery of Grace
cases.synopsis: The Committee on Judicial Business found Case 2, the complaint of TE Hathaway et al. against the Presbytery of Grace, to be in order and recommended that a commission be appointed to adjudicate the matter.

cases.title: Knight v. Presbytery of Palmetto
cases.synopsis: The Committee on Judicial Business found Case 3, the complaint of RE Knight against the Presbytery of Palmetto, to be in order and recommended that a commission be appointed to adjudicate the matter.

cases.title: Steele v. Presbytery of Great Lakes
cases.synopsis: The Committee on Judicial Business found Case 4, the appeal of TE Steele against the Presbytery of Great Lakes, to be in order and recommended that a commission be appointed; a constitutional inquiry was appended regarding whether the appellant's divestiture from office was suspended pending the commission's action.

cases.title: Abeel et al. v. Presbytery of James River
cases.synopsis: The Committee on Judicial Business found Case 5, the appeal of RE Abeel et al. against the Presbytery of James River, to be in order and recommended that a commission be appointed to adjudicate the matter.

cases.title: Shell v. Presbytery of Great Lakes
cases.synopsis: The Committee on Judicial Business found Case 6, the complaint of TE Shell against the Presbytery of Great Lakes, not to be in order because an appeal (BCO 14-25) was the proper remedy in the matter, precluding a complaint under BCO 43-1; if the assembly found the appeal not in order, the committee recommended the complaint then be found in order.

cases.title: Dye et al. v. Presbytery of Missouri
cases.synopsis: The Committee on Judicial Business found Case 7, the six complaints of RE Dye et al. against the Presbytery of Missouri, to be in order and recommended that a single commission be appointed to adjudicate all six complaints.
